Title: From metabolic fingerprints to field solutions: engineering the apple rhizosphere microbiome via host-directed Bacillus recruitment for sustainable apple replant disease control

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling/metabolite efflux associated with recruitment of protective Bacillus and suppression of apple replant disease
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots after Fusarium proliferatum MR5 inoculation; higher in resistant rootstock CG935 than sensitive rootstock M9T337
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root/rhizosphere-associated expression induced by Fpmd MR5 stress; significantly higher in CG935
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium proliferatum f. sp. malus domestica MR5 inoculation; apple replant disease stress
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for lysine production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher MdDHDPS expression is associated with increased rhizosphere lysine content and enhanced recruitment of Bacillus, contributing to reduced apple replant disease damage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdDHDPS transcript abundance increased after Fpmd MR5 inoculation in both rootstocks. 2. Induction was stronger in resistant CG935 than in sensitive M9T337. 3. MdDHDPS expression positively correlated with rhizosphere lysine content and Bacillus OTU1748 abundance, linking host lysine biosynthesis to beneficial microbe recruitment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR/expression quantification; rhizosphere metabolite profiling; correlation analysis with lysine and Bacillus OTU1748 abundance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key/rate-limiting enzyme by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA microbiome sequencing, NCBI SRA PRJNA1255861; non-targeted metabolomics, MetaboLights MTBLS12530
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to enhance lysine-mediated recruitment of beneficial Bacillus in rootstocks for sustainable apple replant disease control; suggested for future plant-level overexpression/perturbation tests
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to dihydrodipicolinate synthase as DHDPS in text and MdDHPS/MdDHDPS in figure legend/body; role inferred as host lysine biosynthetic enzyme driving rhizosphere lysine efflux.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling/metabolite efflux associated with recruitment of protective Bacillus and suppression of apple replant disease
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early entry step into aspartate-derived lysine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots after Fpmd MR5 inoculation; higher in resistant rootstock CG935 than sensitive rootstock M9T337
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root/rhizosphere-associated expression induced by Fpmd MR5 stress; significantly higher in CG935
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium proliferatum f. sp. malus domestica MR5 inoculation; apple replant disease stress
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for lysine production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher MdAK expression is associated with increased rhizosphere lysine content and enhanced recruitment of Bacillus, contributing to disease suppression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdAK expression increased after Fpmd MR5 inoculation in both rootstocks. 2. Induction was stronger in resistant CG935 than in sensitive M9T337. 3. MdAK expression positively correlated with Bacillus OTU1748 abundance and rhizosphere lysine content, supporting host metabolic control of beneficial microbiome recruitment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR/expression quantification; rhizosphere metabolite profiling; correlation analysis with lysine and Bacillus OTU1748 abundance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key/rate-limiting enzyme by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA microbiome sequencing, NCBI SRA PRJNA1255861; non-targeted metabolomics, MetaboLights MTBLS12530
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to engineer rootstock lysine biosynthesis and exudation for Bacillus recruitment-based apple replant disease management
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Abstract describes aspartate kinase and DHDPS as key rate-limiting enzymes upregulated 2.79–6.81-fold in resistant rootstocks relative to sensitive material under infection.
